Lord Ed Vaizey

Lord Ed Vaizey

Business Advisor, Broadcaster, Speaker and Member of the House of Lords

Biography

Ed Vaizey is a member of the House of Lords, business adviser, broadcaster, speaker and event host.

Ed was the Member of Parliament for Wantage between 2005 and 2019. He served as the UK Government Culture and Digital Minister from 2010-16, and is the longest-serving Minister in that role. He was appointed a privy councillor in 2016.

In his role as digital minister, Ed was responsible for the roll out of the successful rural broadband programme to more than 4 million homes, the introduction of 4G, and tax credits for film, television, animation and video games, which helped make the creative industries the fastest growing part of the UK economy.

Ed also helped persuade Lucas Films to make the new Star Wars in the UK, and received a credit in the first film of the series.

Ed published the first White Paper on Culture for 50 years, in 2016.

As well as his work in the House of Lords, Ed advises a number of companies on strategic issues. He is a well known broadcaster, with a show on Times Radio, and regularly contributes to the media.

Ed is a trustee of Tate, chairman of the UK-ASEAN Business Council, an honorary fellow of the Radio Academy, and has an honorary degree from Newcastle University, where he is a visiting professor.

Speaking & Events

Ed is regularly hired to speak at important conferences and events.  Here is a sample of some of his most recent engagements:

Enders media and telecoms conference

Ed chaired a panel on AI with two senior executives in the space – head of EMEA for Google, Matt Brittin, and founder and investor Sherry Coutu CBE

Keynote speech on future of FinTech in ASEAN forum

Ed gave a speech at the #ASEAN Future of Fintech Forum where he stressed the leading role the UK can continue to play to support the development of ASEAN’s FinTech sector.

Hosted panel at Cole & Son, with the head of design for Aston Martin

Ed hosted a panel for Cole & Son, the venerable wallpaper brand, on the importance of design, with the chief creative officer of Aston Martin Marek Reichman, and Alexandra Llewellyn the founder of a luxury board games business, in front of an exclusive, invited audience.

Keynote speaker at World E-commerce Forum in Istanbul

Ed spoke recently in Istanbul as the keynote speaker at the World E-Commerce Forum, where he addressed key technology issues affecting the development of e-commerce

Business Advisory

Ed works with a range of clients advising them on their key strategic issues.

House of Lords

Watch Lord Vaizey’s recent interventions in the House of Lords on subjects pertaining to culture, technology, media, and heritage.

Speech in the House of Lords on recognising the importance of the arts

Speech in the House of Lords on the Media Bill and diversity in media

View on Linkedin

Debating the pros and cons of AI regulation in the House of Lords.

View on Linkedin

Speech in the House of Lords about how Ofcom proceeds with regulation and deals potential breaches in conduct.

View on Linkedin

Commending Lord Dominic Johnson for making moves to encourage inward investment from large foreign philanthropic foundations.

View on Linkedin

Asking the Foreign Secretary, David Cameron to consider a comprehensive cultural diplomacy strategy for United Kingdom.

View on Linkedin

Media

Ed is a well-known broadcaster, podcaster and media commentator.  He host a regular Friday evening show on Times Radio, a New UK company, on Friday evenings, covering business and culture.  He has also hosted a number of podcast series, including one on technology with media bank LionTree, one on culture with Country and Townhouse magazine, and one on digital skills with Digital Futures.
